resolution: FIXED + user-verified. routes/downloadManager.js _startRemoteDownload ATTACH guard now prefers the fresh remoteModelsCheck truth (statusResults[dep.id].installed) over a cached depJob.status==='complete' — a dep the volume no longer has can no longer short-circuit the re-install. Pre-check failure falls back to cache. Shipped commit ac1c308 (RunPod); user confirmed the re-install fix verified this session. App-only, no image. NOTE: a SEPARATE renderer-side dead-button-after-uninstall bug (frontend state.downloadJobs stale 'complete') was found in the same session and carded as MPI-102 — that is NOT this backend fix.
subject: Live bug in your downloadManager.js territory: remote re-install after uninstall is a stale-_depJobs false-positive (no download, instant INSTALLED, no Uninstall button)
body:
Found this live while verifying MPI-99 on the no-GPU Pod (p3tayimai7po9t). It is NOT my dialog fix and NOT the disk gate — it lives in _startRemoteDownload's MPI-97 in-flight ATTACH, which is in the function you are actively editing, so flagging instead of touching your file.

REPRO (live, remote mode): earlier this session 4 models installed concurrently, so every dep got depJob.status='complete' in the module-level _depJobs Map. User then UNINSTALLED ill-anime with deleteFiles=true — log confirms 'remote uninstall ill-anime: removed 1 ... model files (deleteFiles=true)' and the Pod volume dropped 79GB→72GB (weight really gone). User then pressed INSTALL on ill-anime: it flashed a progress bar and instantly showed green INSTALLED, volume stayed at 72GB (nothing downloaded), app.log shows NO install line at all, and the card now has NO Uninstall button.

ROOT CAUSE (routes/downloadManager.js _startRemoteDownload, ~line 874): the ATTACH guard is
  const inFlight = _remoteDepIds.has(dep.id) || depJob.status === 'downloading' || depJob.status === 'complete';
  if (inFlight) continue;  // not added to toInstall, no wrapper install
The uninstall path does NOT reset the stale _depJobs[depId].status from 'complete' back to queued/removed. So on re-install the dep reads inFlight=true from the PRIOR session's completion → skipped → toInstall ends empty → no /wrapper/models/install fired → instant settle → card renders installed:true while the file is gone from the volume. The missing Uninstall button is a downstream render symptom of that false installed:true.

Note: the up-front remoteModelsCheck pre-check is INNOCENT — wrapper _is_complete_on_disk (wrapper.py ~779) checks real file existence+size and would correctly report the deleted weight as NOT installed. The stale state is purely the app-side _depJobs Map, bypassed by the ATTACH continue before statusResults is even consulted.

SUGGESTED FIX (your call, your file): on uninstall, clear/reset the affected dep ids in _depJobs (drop them or set status back so they are not seen 'complete'), AND/OR in the ATTACH guard prefer the fresh statusResults[dep.id].installed over the cached depJob.status==='complete' (don't let a stale 'complete' short-circuit a dep the volume no longer has). Reproduce on the same Pod: install a model, uninstall it (delete files), re-install — expect a real download, not instant INSTALLED.

My MPI-99 MpiModal dialog fix is verified+separate (first uninstall opened the dialog after reload). I am NOT editing downloadManager.js — it's yours.

recent_events.note: MPI-100 owner. Fixed in routes/downloadManager.js _startRemoteDownload: ATTACH guard now prefers fresh remoteModelsCheck (statusResults[dep.id].installed) over a cached depJob.status==='complete' — a stale 'complete' for a dep the volume no longer has can no longer short-circuit the re-install. statusResults absent (pre-check failed) falls back to cache. node --check OK; decision-logic test 6/6. App-only, no image. Will resolve after USER live-verify on p3tayimai7po9t (install -> uninstall deleteFiles -> re-install expects a real download).
User-verified this session; MPI-100 closed. Separate dead-button-after-uninstall renderer bug carded as MPI-102.
